Title: Flavonoid deficiency disrupts redox homeostasis and terpenoid biosynthesis in glandular trichomes of tomato

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: af mutant (LA1049); CHI4 and CHI9 complemented lines
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; nucleus; reticulate pattern consistent with cytosolic face of ER
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CHI1 strongly reduces rutin/flavonoid accumulation and causes severe depletion of mono- and sesquiterpene volatiles in glandular trichomes; complementation restores both flavonoids and terpenoids.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (af); stable complementation with pCHI1::CHI1; CHI1-GFP and CHI1-GFP-GUS localization/complementation; RNA-seq; quantitative proteomics; GC-MS; HPLC-MS; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI1 deficiency in af downregulated many terpenoid-biosynthetic genes and reduced several pathway enzymes in tVI-GTs. 2. af trichomes accumulated <10% of wild-type terpene volatiles and had strongly reduced rutin, while naringenin chalcone accumulated. 3. Complementation with native-promoter CHI1 restored rutin and β-phellandrene. 4. Nuclear exclusion of CHI1 did not impair restoration of terpenoids, indicating the canonical flavonoid-pathway function, not a nuclear moonlighting role, underlies the phenotype. 5. CHI1 deficiency correlated with elevated ROS and altered fatty-acid homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in type VI glandular trichomes; native promoter active in gland cells
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Variable light intensity (100, 200, 500 μE m^-2 s^-1) examined for metabolite correlations
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of isolated tVI-GTs, NCBI GEO GSE118065; quantitative proteomics (TMT-based), accession NA; metabolomics (GC-MS, HPLC-MS)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Terpenoid volatiles contribute to glandular trichome specialized metabolism and anti-herbivore defense; flavonoids contribute to antioxidant/ROS-scavenging and photoprotection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CHI1 associates with reduced trichome terpene volatiles, elevated ROS, stress-response activation, and reduced trichome density reported for af
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CHI1 is a leverage point for maintaining both flavonoid-based redox buffering and glandular-trichome terpene output; useful for engineering defensive volatile production without requiring nuclear targeting.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although CHI1 is an enzyme in the flavonoid pathway, the paper primarily establishes it as a decisive non-transcriptional determinant linking flavonoid status to terpenoid output in trichomes; authors rule out requirement for nuclear localization.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: af mutant (LA1049); CHI4 and CHI9 complemented lines
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; nucleus; associated with cytosolic face of ER
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Early step, upstream of flavonol/end-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes intramolecular cyclization/isomerization of naringenin chalcone to the flavanone naringenin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of flavonoid end products and indirectly required for robust accumulation of terpenoid volatiles in glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for flavonoid flux in this study; loss abolishes downstream flavonoid flux in af trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: rutin; volatile mono- and sesquiterpenes (e.g., β-phellandrene, β-caryophyllene)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of CHI1 causes strong depletion of rutin and trichome terpenoid volatiles, with accumulation of the substrate naringenin chalcone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: af mutant; complementation with CHI1; HPLC-MS flavonoid profiling; GC-MS terpene profiling; RNA-seq; proteomics; confocal localization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. af mutant lacking CHI1 accumulated high naringenin chalcone and low downstream flavonoids. 2. CHI1 complementation restored rutin accumulation. 3. CHI1 deficiency correlated with downregulation of MEP/MVA/terpene synthase genes and severe loss of trichome terpenoids. 4. Excluding CHI1 from the nucleus did not block restoration of β-phellandrene, supporting a metabolic rather than nuclear regulatory mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light intensity variation tested across genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of isolated tVI-GTs, GEO GSE118065; TMT proteomics; metabolomics by HPLC-MS and GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids support antioxidant protection and photoprotection; trichome terpenoids contribute to defensive volatile production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CHI1 deficiency elevates ROS and perturbs fatty-acid accumulation and stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering or preserving CHI1 function can sustain flavonoid flux and indirectly protect terpene productivity in glandular trichomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study indicates the impact on terpenoids is indirect through flavonoid-dependent redox homeostasis rather than direct terpenoid catalysis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: af mutant; CHI4 and CHI9 controls
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond, converting 18:1 to 18:2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies/raises unsaturated fatty acid pool rather than the paper's main terminal nutrient product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript abundance of SlFAD2-1 was modestly but significantly higher in af trichomes, consistent with increased 18:2 and elevated polyunsaturated fatty acid content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; GC-MS fatty acid quantification; genome-scale metabolic modeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc01g006430 was the overwhelmingly predominant expressed SlFAD transcript in tVI-GTs. 2. Its transcript level was significantly increased in af. 3. af trichomes accumulated higher total fatty acids, especially 18:2 and 18:3, consistent with enhanced unsaturated fatty-acid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expressed among seven detected SlFAD homologs in tVI-GTs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of isolated tVI-GTs, GEO GSE118065; fatty-acid metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Unsaturated fatty acids may contribute to ROS quenching and lipid homeostasis in trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher unsaturated fatty acids in af coincide with elevated ROS stress; causal protective benefit not directly proven
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning trichome lipid unsaturation/redox buffering, though not a primary target product gene in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results section links its altered expression to measured nutrient-relevant fatty-acid changes, but it is secondary to CHI1.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS and other flavonoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of CHS and other flavonoid pathway genes under UV-B response context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UVR8 protein and transcript levels increased in af trichomes, consistent with stress-responsive upregulation of flavonoid biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; quantitative proteomics; prior functional literature cited in paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UVR8 accumulated to higher levels in af trichomes at both transcript/protein levels. 2. The increase coincided with upregulation of CHS and other flavonoid biosynthetic genes. 3. Authors discuss UVR8 as a candidate stress-responsive factor contributing to compensatory flavonoid-gene induction.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B/light stress context; flavonoid deficiency in af
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of isolated tVI-GTs, GEO GSE118065; TMT proteomics
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids provide photoprotection and antioxidant activity
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible regulator for boosting stress-induced flavonoid production, but not directly validated here as causal driver of target metabolite change.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive in this paper; presented as an attractive candidate for compensatory induction of flavonoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in Results with measured upaccumulation, but not functionally manipulated here; therefore secondary/core-supporting rather than primary focal gene.
